data.title: Adobe rises after HSBC upgrades to Buy
data.description:
HSBC upgraded Adobe (ADBE) to Buy from Hold and increased the price target to $308 from $282, citing limited AI disruption.

Shares of Adobe rose about 2% premarket on Thursday.

Analysts led by Stephen Bersey said that recalling that Adobe’s fiscal second quarter revenue rose 12.7% year-over-year and that the company guided to fiscal 2026 revenue growth of 11.8%, they have yet to see any material impact from AI-powered competitors.

"Adobe maintains strong revenue growth even though AI design tools were some of the first major AI applications available in the market and there have been concerns these will displace traditional software products such as Adobe’s," said Bersey and his team. "Adobe’s platform has proved to be 'sticky' due to user workflow familiarity and new AI-powered features (both Adobe and third-party tools) being embedded, in our view. Consequently, we believe the market is overestimating the adverse impact of AI-based design tools and that reward now outweighs risks, and recent results continue to be strong."

The analysts noted that total remaining performance obligations, or RPO, and current RPO rose 13.1% year-over-year in the fiscal second quarter, and after a post-pandemic surge in fiscal 2021, Adobe’s revenue and cRPO growth have settled at a low double-digit Compound Annual Growth Rate, or CAGR, and show no signs of disruption due to AI thus far. 

"Adobe is monetizing AI, with AI first revenue rising 3x y-o-y, though it was still only c2% of 2QFY26 revenue. Ironically this suggests that, even within Adobe’s installed base, switching to new AI-powered features is not a “replacement” to users’ normal work patterns," said the analysts.

data.title: China Yuchai declares $0.87 dividend
data.description:
• China Yuchai (CYD) declares $0.87/share annual dividend.
  • Forward yield 1.88%
  • Payable July 28; for shareholders of record July 17; ex-div July 17.
  • See CYD Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Constellation Brands cut to ‘Hold’ at Freedom Broker
data.description:
• Freedom Broker downgraded Constellation Brands (STZ) to “Hold” from “Buy” and cut its price target to $173 from $223, citing softer beer depletion trends and limited near-term visibility despite an otherwise strong fiscal first-quarter performance.
  • New target implies a 24.6% upside from the stock’s last close of $138.88.

  • The brokerage said the company beat consensus estimates in the quarter with beer net sales rising 2%, but depletions slipped 0.3% after a 0.6% gain in the prior quarter, which management attributed to broader consumer pressure, while wine & spirits organic net sales increased 8%, rebounding from a 6% decline in the previous quarter.

  • It noted Constellation raised its FY27 reported EPS guidance while keeping its comparable EPS outlook unchanged but flagged higher marketing spending in the second and third quarters as a margin headwind.

data.title: Palantir rises on back of DA Davidson upgrade
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) was in focus on Thursday as DA Davidson upgraded the enterprise software company to Buy from Neutral and upped its price target to $175 from $165.

Shares rose 3.5% in premarket trading.

“Palantir has several competitive advantages over all other software companies, which is becoming more pronounced in the era of AI,” analyst Gil Luria wrote in a note to clients. “Most recently, the realization by enterprise customers that an orchestration layer is necessary accentuates the need for Palantir.”

Delving deeper, Luria said that recent developments have shown why customers will use Palantir's software to help orchestrate artificial intelligence models.

“Anthropic's repeated choice to take a confrontational tact with the US government has resulted in the government placing restrictions on AI models and Anthropic pulling its own model from the market,” Luria added. “If an enterprise customer built its business directly on top of these models, the disruption to their business could have been catastrophic. In contrast, a company that built its business on an orchestration tool, such as with Palantir, would only experience a minor transition as Palantir swaps the AI models underneath its solution.”

data.title: Tesla beats Q2 delivery estimates
data.description:
• Tesla (TSLA) delivered 480,126 vehicles in the second quarter, beating estimates of 406K deliveries and briefly driving shares higher into Thursday’s open.
  • The company produced 451,758 units.
  • In the second quarter, Tesla (TSLA) also said it deployed 13.5 GWh of energy storage products.

data.title: U.S. jobless claims fall to 215K, beating estimates as labor market holds firm
data.description:
U.S. initial jobless claims fell to 215K for the week ended June 27, beating the 220K consensus estimate and coming in slightly below the prior week's revised 216K, according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

The latest reading suggests that layoffs remain limited despite higher interest rates and broader economic uncertainty, pointing to continued resilience in the labor market.

The four-week moving average of initial claims, which helps smooth weekly volatility, declined by 2.5K to 222K from the prior week's revised average of 224.5K.

Meanwhile, continuing claims, which track the number of people already receiving unemployment benefits, rose modestly to 1.814M for the week ended June 20 from a revised 1.812M in the previous week.

The four-week average for continuing claims increased by 10.75K to 1.803M, indicating some workers are taking longer to find new jobs even as layoffs remain subdued.

The seasonally adjusted insured unemployment rate held steady at 1.2%, unchanged from the previous week.

On an unadjusted basis, actual initial claims under state programs totaled 213,550, up 5,545 or 2.7% from the previous week but below the 230,392 claims recorded during the same period last year.

Overall, the data points to a stable U.S. labor market with no signs of widespread layoffs.

data.title: Novo reportedly seeking discounts from suppliers to cut costs
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) is said to have written to suppliers seeking discounts to lower its cost base as the Danish drugmaker tries to regain leadership in the weight-loss drug market currently dominated by Eli Lilly (LLY).

In a statement, the company confirmed the move, noting that it was implemented to ensure its partnerships “remain commercially sustainable,” Bloomberg reported.

“We must ensure that our cost base remains sustainable to support long-term competitiveness, innovation, and our ability to continue serving patients,” Novo (NVO) said. “While participation is not mandatory, we take a positive response into account as part of our broader supplier assessment.”

However, Danish business publication Finans, which first reported the news, stated that some suppliers have regarded Novo’s (NVO) communication as a warning to their future dealings with the company.

In the GLP-1 market, Lilly (LLY) leads with a 60% share while Novo (NVO) trails with a 40% share as of Q1 2026. In September, the company announced plans to eliminate approximately 9,000 jobs globally as part of a cost-cutting drive initiated under new CEO Mike Doustdar.

data.title: Amazon's midnight Leo launch allows Starlink competitor to go live later this year
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) successfully completed a launch just after midnight on Thursday, adding 29 more Leo satellites into low orbit, bringing its total deployed to 396, which puts the company on track to begin service later this year.

Leo is Amazon's satellite internet network, which is built to deliver high-speed, low-latency broadband to residences, businesses and remote locations around the globe. It will be the most direct competitor to SpaceX's (SPCX) Starlink, which has quite a head start as it went live in 2020.

"Last few launches were big for Amazon Leo - bringing us to 390+ satellites deployed, enough to support continuous service across initial latitudes," Chris Weber, vice president of Amazon Leo business, posted on X early Thursday morning. "Still lots of work ahead—including raising all these new satellites to their assigned altitude—but we've completed enough launches for initial service this year, and future missions just add coverage and capacity."

Starlink started with nearly 900 satellites in low-Earth orbit and has since ballooned that figure to more than 10,000, providing service to about 160 countries. Amazon plans to eventually have about 3,200 Leo satellites.

"With hundreds of flight-ready satellites standing by at the Cape and a new, dedicated vertical integration facility ready to support Leo Vulcan 1 and subsequent missions, we have a clear path to increase launch and deployment cadence, helping us quickly expand network coverage following an initial service rollout later this year," said Melissa Wuerl, Amazon Leo's director of launch systems.

data.title: Kartoon Studios adopts shareholder rights plan
data.description:
• Kartoon Studios (TOON)  said on Thursday its board unanimously adopted a limited-duration shareholder rights plan and declared a dividend of one right for each outstanding common share.
  • The record date for the rights distribution is July 13. The company said the plan was not adopted in response to any specific takeover bid or other proposal to acquire control of the company.

data.title: US stock futures move higher after June jobs report
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved higher Thursday after June’s nonfarm payrolls report came in weaker than economists had expected.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) rise 0.48%, S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged higher by 0.37% and Dow futures (INDU) rose 0.44%.

The U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs in June, falling short of the 115,000 increase economists surveyed by Dow Jones had expected. The unemployment rate, however, unexpectedly declined to 4.2%, versus forecasts for it to hold at 4.3%.

Treasury yields edged higher across key benchmarks. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 0.05 basis points to 4.19%, the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed 1.1 basis points to 4.50%, and the longer-term 30-year yield (US30Y) moved up 1.3 basis points to 4.99%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Aon (AON) +7.52%, Cintas (CTAS) +4.43%, and KKR (KKR) +4.24%. Decliners included SanDisk (SNDK) -3.85%, and Dell Technologies (DELL) -3.34%.
